zh.Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, Englan: 英國越可爾普倫普頓 在 斯波福斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nigel le Boteler於1253年出生在英國越可爾普倫普頓 在 斯波福斯時，他的父親Ralph le Boteler of Oversley33歲，母親Matilda Pantulf27歲。他於1272年之前與Agnes在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1334年之後在英國英格蘭戴文普林普頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nigel le Boteler于1253年出生在英国越可爾普倫普頓 在 斯波福斯时，他的父亲Ralph le Boteler of Oversley33岁，母亲Matilda Pantulf27岁。他于1272年之前与Agnes在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1334年之后在英国英格兰戴文普林普頓。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Nigel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the medieval name Nigellus, a Latinized version (ostensibly representing a diminutive of Latin niger ‘black’) of the vernacular Ni(h)el, i.e. Neil . Although it is frequently found in medieval records, this form was probably not used in everyday life before its revival by antiquarians such as Sir Walter Scott in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Nigel le Boteler was born in 1253, in Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, England, his father, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, was 33 and his mother, Matilda Pantulf, was 27. He married Agnes before 1272, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died after 1334, in Plympton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nigel le Boteler est né en 1253, à Plumpton in Spofforth, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Matilda Pantulf, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Agnes avant 1272, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé après 1334, à Plympton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nigel le Boteler 1253 in Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph le Boteler of Oversley 33 und seine Mutter Matilda Pantulf 27. Er hat vor 1272 in Europa Agnes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1334 in Plympton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nigel le Boteler è nato il nel 1253 nel Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Pantulf, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Agnes prima del 1272, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1334, nel Plympton, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Nigel le Botelerが1253年にイギリスヨークシュアイアプルンプトン イン スプオフォースで生まれた時，父Ralph le Boteler of Oversleyは33歳，母Matilda Pantulfは27歳であった。1272年以前にヨーロッパでAgnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1334年以後にイギリスイギリスデボンプリンプトンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nigel le Boteler(이)가 1253년 영국 요르크실르 플럼프턴 인 스포포스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph le Boteler of Oversley(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Pantulf(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1272년 이전 우럽에서 Agnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1334년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 플림프턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nigel le Boteler nasceu em 1253, em Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Pantulf, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes antes 1272, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu após 1334, em Plympton, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nigel le Boteler родился в 1253 г. в местности плумптон ин споффорт, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph le Boteler of Oversley было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda Pantulf, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes перед 1272 г. в местности Европа. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер после 1334 г. в местности плимптон, девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nigel le Boteler nació en 1253, en Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Matilda Pantulf, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Agnes antes de 1272, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió después de 1334, en Plympton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
